templates.name: Plan-Based AI Workflow
templates.description: Structured AI development with plans, phases, and validation
templates.abstract: Provides a complete AI-assisted development workflow with investigation plans, phased implementation, and human-in-the-loop validation. Includes CLAUDE.md, plan templates, and git safety rules.
templates.summary: A structured AI development workflow that guides AI coding assistants through investigation, planning, and phased implementation. Includes CLAUDE.md, 6 portable docs, plan templates, and git safety rules. Designed for human-in-the-loop collaboration.

templates.name: C# Basic Webserver
templates.description: ASP.NET Core server with health endpoint and Docker support
templates.abstract: Provides a minimal starting point for developers who want to build a C# web server using ASP.NET Core.
templates.summary: A minimal ASP.NET Core web server with hot reload via dotnet watch, Docker multi-stage build, Kubernetes deployment manifests, and GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow.

quickstart.title: Run the ASP.NET Core app
quickstart.note: ASP.NET Core runs on port 3000 with hot reload via dotnet watch. VS Code auto-forwards the port.

resolvedTools.name: C# Development Tools
resolvedTools.description: Installs .NET SDK, ASP.NET Core Runtime, and VS Code extensions for C# development

templates.name: Designsystemet Basic React App
templates.description: React app with Designsystemet components, Vite, and TypeScript
templates.abstract: Provides a minimal starting point for developers who want to build a blog page using Designsystemet's components and styling with React/Vite.
templates.summary: A React application using Designsystemet from Digdir with blog cards, Vite for development, TypeScript support, Docker containerization, Kubernetes deployment manifests, and GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow.

quickstart.title: Run the Vite dev server
quickstart.note: Vite runs on port 5173. VS Code auto-forwards the port — click the globe icon in the Ports tab.

resolvedTools.name: TypeScript Development Tools
resolvedTools.description: Adds TypeScript and development tools (Node.js already in devcontainer)

templates.name: Python Basic Webserver
templates.description: Minimal Flask server with health endpoint and Docker support
templates.abstract: Provides a minimal starting point for developers who want to build a Python web server using Flask.
templates.summary: A minimal Python web server using Flask with a health check endpoint, Docker containerization, Kubernetes deployment manifests, and GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow. Ideal for microservices and API backends.

quickstart.title: Run the Flask app
quickstart.note: Flask runs on port 3000. VS Code auto-forwards the port — click the globe icon in the Ports tab.

resolvedTools.name: Python Development Tools
resolvedTools.description: Adds ipython, pytest-cov, uv, and VS Code extensions for Python development

templates.name: Python Basic Webserver with Database
templates.description: Flask server that connects to PostgreSQL and reads from a tasks table
templates.abstract: A minimal Flask web server that connects to a PostgreSQL database and reads from a tasks table. Demonstrates the full producer/consumer flow — UIS deploys PostgreSQL, dev-template configure creates the database and wires the connection into .env, and the app reads from it.
templates.summary: A Flask web server that reads from a PostgreSQL database via DATABASE_URL. Includes a sample tasks table, Docker containerization, Kubernetes deployment manifests, and GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow. This is the consumer-side companion to the postgresql-demo UIS stack template — run that first to deploy PostgreSQL, then dev-template configure on this template to create the database and wire up the connection.

quickstart.title: Run the Flask app
quickstart.note:
Flask debug server starts on port 3000.
VS Code auto-forwards the port — click the globe icon in the Ports tab.

resolvedTools.name: Python Development Tools
resolvedTools.description: Adds ipython, pytest-cov, uv, and VS Code extensions for Python development

resolvedInitFiles.config/init-database.sql:
-- Init file for python-basic-webserver-database
-- Applied by: uis configure postgresql --init-file -
-- Uses psql --set ON_ERROR_STOP=on for fail-fast on syntax errors
-- All statements are idempotent — safe to re-run

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);

INSERT INTO tasks (title, status) VALUES
    ('Set up the database connection', 'done'),
    ('Build something with Flask + PostgreSQL', 'pending'),
    ('Deploy to Kubernetes via ArgoCD', 'pending')
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

templates.name: PostgreSQL Demo
templates.description: Deploys PostgreSQL and creates a sample database with seed data
templates.abstract: A minimal UIS template that deploys PostgreSQL and creates a sample tasks table with seed data. Use this to verify UIS template deployment end-to-end, or as a starting point for your own stack templates.
templates.summary: A minimal demonstration template that deploys PostgreSQL to the UIS cluster and creates a sample database with a tasks table and seed data. Shows the uis template flow from registry to deployed, configured service. Use it to verify your UIS setup or as a starting point for your own stack templates.

quickstart.title: Verify the database
quickstart.note:
Inside psql, run: SELECT * FROM tasks;
You should see 3 seeded rows. Type \q to exit.

resolvedInitFiles.config/init-database.sql:
-- PostgreSQL Demo — sample schema with seed data
-- Applied by: uis configure postgresql --init-file -
-- Uses psql --set ON_ERROR_STOP=on for fail-fast on syntax errors
-- All statements are idempotent — safe to re-run

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);

INSERT INTO tasks (title, status) VALUES
    ('Verify uis template install works', 'done'),
    ('Build your own UIS stack template', 'pending'),
    ('Deploy it with uis template install', 'pending')
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
